Read this if it sounds familiar

If your business keeps growing, but it still depends on you too much, Velocity is for you.

A lot of founders think they have a growth problem. They do not. They have a structure problem.

Revenue comes in, but it still depends on you.

The team is there, but key decisions still land on your desk.

The offers sell, but the business still needs the founder to hold everything together.

The brand has momentum, but the model is still too dependent on personal delivery and personal energy.

So the business grows, but the founder feels heavier, not freer. That is the problem Velocity is built to solve.

Velocity is not a coaching group. It is not a networking club. It is not a place to collect more notes and hope something changes.

Methodology

Inside Velocity, we use the 50 Shades of Paid methodology and the full canvas sequence to diagnose real bottlenecks, choose the right path of expansion, and build a stronger revenue structure.

Who Velocity is for

Velocity is for the founder who has already built something real.

You have traction. You have clients. You have demand. You have proof that the market wants what you do. But you can also see the truth.

The business still needs too much from you. You are still too central. Too many things still move because of your effort, your presence, your approvals, your ideas, your visibility, or your personal involvement.

You are not looking for

  • Beginner coaching
  • More noise
  • Generic accountability
  • Inspirational sessions that change nothing

You are looking for

  • A serious room
  • A stronger model
  • Strategic depth
  • A better business on the other side of nine months
What Velocity helps you do

This is not about working harder.
It is about building smarter.

Velocity helps you identify what is keeping the business dependent on you, decide what the next version of the business actually needs to look like, and redesign the revenue structure so the company can grow without asking more and more from the founder every time.

01

Where revenue is fragile

Surface the parts of the model that look healthy but cannot hold weight.

02

Where authority is stuck

Locate the positioning gaps that keep growth capped at founder capacity.

03

Where the business breaks under growth

Find the structural pressure points before scale exposes them publicly.

04

Which model you should be building next

Choose the right path of expansion instead of chasing more complexity.

05

How offers and delivery need to evolve

Redesign monetization so it reflects the business you are building, not the one you started.

06

What you need to stop carrying

Separate what belongs to you from what the business itself should now hold.

What happens inside Velocity

Six structured ways we do the work together.

01 . Monthly mastermind sessions

These are not teaching calls.
These are decision rooms.

Each month, we meet in a private mastermind session focused on the real decisions shaping the next version of your business.

This is where we look at what is happening in the business now, what is causing drag, what needs to change, and what the smartest next move is.

02 . Monthly canvas labs

Where strategy gets translated into structure.

Each month we also work through the methodology inside a dedicated Canvas Lab. We use the canvases to diagnose, clarify, and design the business more intentionally.

This is where the work becomes concrete.

03 . One private intensive day

A focused working day dedicated to your business.

Every member receives one private intensive day devoted to your constraints, your opportunities, and the major decisions that need to be made.

We use this day to go deeper, move faster, and solve what should not be left to chance.

3
Guaranteed reviews
04 . Three guaranteed founder reviews

You are not left figuring out major inflection points on your own.

Every founder receives three direct strategic reviews across the nine months. One during diagnosis. One during decision. One during design.

These are built in checkpoints for the decisions that matter most.

Private member channel
A focused space for serious founders doing serious work.
05 . Private member channel

Questions, accountability, reflection, and real support between sessions.

You also get access to a private member channel for the ongoing conversation that happens between formal sessions.

This is not a crowded community feed. It is a focused room for a small number of serious founders.

06 . Private Dallas founder retreat

One private in person retreat in Dallas.

An intimate founder dinner and a focused weekend of strategy, recalibration, and business design.

This is not a social event. This is a working room. It is where we step back from the pace of the business, look at what is really happening, and make the decisions that are hard to make when you are in the middle of the day to day.

What we work through during the nine months

Three phases. One better business.

Velocity moves in a deliberate sequence. You do not jump to building before you know what needs to change, and you do not redesign the model before the right decisions have been made.

i.
Phase One
Diagnose

We identify what is really creating pressure in the business. Where is the founder still the bottleneck. Where is revenue still fragile. Where is the business stronger on the surface than it is underneath. Where are the real leaks. Where does growth already expose weakness.

ii.
Phase Two
Decide

We decide what path of growth actually makes sense. Not every business needs the same model. We identify what to build, what to stop, what to package, what to shift, and what the next version of the business actually needs. Choosing direction, not chasing complexity.

iii.
Phase Three
Design

We redesign the revenue structure. This is where your monetization, offers, delivery model, and growth pathway get rebuilt with more intention. By the end of this phase, you do not just have more ideas. You have a better business.

Why founders choose Velocity

Private work gives you proximity. Velocity gives you a serious room.

Private work is bespoke and one on one. Velocity is different. It gives you the chance to pressure test your business in front of other founders who are dealing with real complexity, real growth, and real responsibility.

It gives you pattern recognition that is hard to get in isolation. It gives you a higher level conversation. It gives you the benefit of seeing your business through the methodology and through the room.

Velocity is not a cheaper version of private work.
It is a different asset.

Velocity is not for

  • Beginners
  • People still trying to figure out if they have a business
  • Founders who want unlimited access
  • People looking for done for you execution
  • Buyers who want hand holding instead of decision making
  • People who want to sit in a room, feel inspired, and leave unchanged

Velocity is for

  • Founders who have already built something real
  • Operators ready to face what the business has outgrown
  • People who want strategic depth, not more noise
  • Leaders who know information is not their problem
  • Founders ready to redesign the model, not just repeat it
  • Those ready to do something about what growth is exposing
What you leave with

By the end of Velocity,
you should leave with more
than insight. You should leave
with a stronger business.

  • A clearer understanding of where the business still depends on you too much
  • A better view of the real revenue ceiling
  • A stronger revenue pathway
  • Better offer structure
  • More intentional monetization
  • Stronger authority placement
  • Cleaner decisions around what you should continue, stop, delegate, systemize, package, or expand
  • A business model more capable of growth without asking more and more from the founder every time

That is the point. Not more information.
Not more motivation. A better business.

This is a private room. Enrollment is limited by design.

Apply for Velocity
Final word

If growth is exposing pressure, weakness, fragility, or founder dependency, do not ignore that.

That is the moment Velocity is built for. Not when you are just getting started. When you already know the business is real, but you can also see that the current structure is too dependent on you.
